meta.title: 8.5 Serverless and FaaS
meta.description: Lambda, Cloud Functions, edge functions. Cold starts, execution limits, and serverless design patterns.

blocks.prompt: You have an API that handles 10 requests/second on average but spikes to 10,000/second during sales events. You pay for servers 24/7 but only need capacity for minutes per day. Is there a better model?

blocks.content:
## Serverless: pay per invocation

Serverless platforms run your code on-demand, scaling automatically from zero to thousands of concurrent executions.

| Platform | Provider | Runtime limit | Cold start |
|---|---|---|---|
| Lambda | AWS | 15 minutes | 100ms-2s |
| Cloud Functions | GCP | 9 minutes | 100ms-1s |
| Azure Functions | Azure | 10 minutes | 100ms-2s |
| Cloudflare Workers | Cloudflare | 30s (free) | <1ms (V8 isolates) |

### Cold starts

When no instances are running, the first request must: provision a container, load your code, and initialize the runtime. This adds 100ms-2s latency.

**Mitigation:** Provisioned concurrency (keep N instances warm), smaller deployment packages, choose faster runtimes (Go, Rust over Java).

### Serverless design patterns

**API + Lambda:** API Gateway → Lambda → DynamoDB. No servers to manage.
**Event processing:** S3 upload → Lambda → process image → save to S3.
**Scheduled tasks:** CloudWatch Events → Lambda. Cron without servers.
**Stream processing:** Kafka/Kinesis → Lambda. Auto-scaling consumers.

### When serverless fits

- Spiky, unpredictable traffic (pay nothing during quiet periods)
- Event-driven processing (file uploads, webhooks, queue consumers)
- APIs with low-medium traffic (<1000 RPS)
- Rapid prototyping (no infrastructure to set up)

### When serverless does NOT fit

- Consistent high traffic (always-on servers are cheaper)
- Long-running processes (>15 minute limit)
- Stateful workloads (WebSocket connections, in-memory caches)
- Sub-10ms latency requirements (cold starts)

Serverless (Lambda, Cloud Functions) scales automatically and charges per invocation — ideal for spiky traffic and event processing. Cold starts add latency (100ms-2s). Not suitable for high-throughput steady-state workloads or long-running processes.

blocks.prompt: Module 8.6 covers the cross-cutting concerns that every production system needs regardless of architecture: observability, configuration, and service discovery.
